title: Identity
text: Given $$sin^2\left(t\right)+cos^2\left(t\right)=1$$, we want to show the other $$2$$ Pythagorean Identities: $$1+{\operatorname{cot}\left(t\right)}^2={\operatorname{csc}\left(t\right)}^2$$ and $$1+tan^2\left(\theta\right)={\operatorname{sec}\left(\theta\right)}^2$$

title: Simplify
text: $$\frac{\sin**2(t)+\cos**2(t)}{\sin**2(t)}=\frac{1}{\sin**2(t)}$$ becomes $$1+\cot**2(t)=\csc**2(t),$$ giving us one of the Pythagorean identities.

title: Simplify
text: $$\frac{\sin**2(t)+\cos**2(t)}{\cos**2(t)}=\frac{1}{\cos**2(t)}$$ becomes $$\tan**2(t)+1=\sec**2(t),$$ giving us another Pythagorean identity.
